channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: PSG Financial Services celebrates top advisers and outlines growth strategy at annual conference
item.description: PSG Financial Services held its annual conference in May under the theme 'Lead the change', bringing together industry leaders, journalists and top advisers to discuss proactive strategies amid geopol...

item.title: Tanzania Approves TZS 2.525 Trillion Energy Budget to Expand Power, Gas, and Rural Electrification
item.description: Tanzania’s Ministry of Energy, under Minister Deogratius Ndejembi, has tabled a TZS 2.525 trillion budget for the 2026/27 financial year, with 97.5% earmarked for development projects. The budget aims...

item.title: Philippine Church calls for peace, support for OFWs, and dialogue during conflicts
item.description: The Catholic Bishops’ Conference of the Philippines (CBCP) has issued a pastoral appeal urging the faithful to pray for peace, support Overseas Filipino Workers (OFWs) and their families, and promote ...

item.title: CNN Fact-Checker Refutes Trump's False Claims About California Election Results
item.description: During a press appearance at Joint Base Andrews on May 20, 2026, President Donald Trump repeated false claims that the U.S. presidential elections in 2016, 2020, and 2024 were 'rigged' against him. Wh...

item.title: Light-Driven Method Produces Strained Housane Molecules for Drug Development
item.description: Researchers at the University of Münster, led by Prof. Frank Glorius, have developed a novel light-driven technique to create highly strained ring-shaped molecules called housanes. These tiny molecule...

item.title: Federal Government Restores Gas Supply to KP CNG Stations and Ensures Wheat Transport Unblocked
item.description: The federal and Khyber Pakhtunkhwa governments have successfully concluded negotiations regarding the closure of CNG stations in the province. Following continuous engagement by KP's leadership, inclu...

item.title: Vigilante Groups Carry Out Violent Attacks on Foreign Nationals in South Africa
item.description: In recent weeks, South Africa has seen a surge of xenophobic violence, primarily targeting African and Asian foreign nationals. Vigilante groups such as March and March have organised demonstrations i...

item.title: Oyo Police Arrest Two Suspects Over Cocoa Institute Abduction
item.description: Police for Oyo State don nab two men, Shehu and Abubakar, wey dem believe say dem get hand for the kidnapping of two staff members from the Cocoa Research Institute of Nigeria, Idi-Ayunre, Oluyole LGA...

item.title: Analyst John Heilemann sees potential Democratic midterm wave based on NYT/Siena poll showing Trump underwater with key voter groups
item.description: On Wednesday, political analyst John Heilemann appeared on MSNBC's 'Morning Joe' to discuss the latest New York Times/Siena College poll, which shows President Donald Trump's approval rating at a reco...

item.title: Jim Jordan questions SPLC funding practices; civil rights attorney defends donor support
item.description: During a House Judiciary Committee hearing, Representative Jim Jordan (R-OH) challenged the Southern Poverty Law Center (SPLC) over its use of donor funds, focusing on allegations that paid informants...

item.title: Jan. 6 Officers File Lawsuit Against Trump-Linked Fund for Capitol Rioters
item.description: Two prominent police officers from the January 6, 2021, Capitol attack have filed a federal lawsuit challenging the Trump administration's $1.776 billion 'Anti-Weaponization Fund.' Former Capitol Poli...

item.title: International Outcry Over Detention of Gaza-Bound Flotilla Activists by Israel
item.description: On May 20, 2026, Israel's far-right National Security Minister Itamar Ben Gvir posted a video showing Gaza-bound flotilla activists detained with their hands tied and kneeling on the deck of an Israel...

item.title: WHO warns Ebola outbreak serious in DRC and neighbouring countries, global risk low
item.description: The World Health Organisation (WHO) don warn say the Ebola outbreak wey dey happen for Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) na serious matter for both national and regional level, even though global...

item.title: Canadian Academic Gad Saad Relocates to the U.S. Amid Safety Concerns and Threats
item.description: Gad Saad, a prominent Canadian academic and author, has announced his permanent move from Montreal to the United States, citing escalating personal safety concerns. Saad, whose book 'Suicidal Empathy'...

item.title: Lagos State recovers over ₦450 million for consumers, sanctions non-compliant businesses
item.description: Lagos State government, through the Lagos State Consumer Protection Agency (LASCOPA), don recover more than ₦450 million for consumers wey suffer from unfair trade practices. Commissioner for Commerce...

item.title: UK Inflation Falls to 2.8% in April Amid Energy Support, But Pressure from Global Events Could Push Prices Higher
item.description: UK inflation has fallen to 2.8% in the year to April, down from 3.3% in March, largely due to lower gas and electricity bills aided by government support and falling wholesale energy costs. Despite th...

item.title: Keating backs CGT changes while Minns calls for bigger income tax relief
item.description: Former Prime Minister Paul Keating has come out in strong support of Treasurer Jim Chalmers and Prime Minister Anthony Albanese following criticism of the recent capital gains tax (CGT) reforms. Keati...

item.title: Bhopal Court Denies Second Autopsy Request in Noida Woman's Death Case
item.description: On Wednesday, a local court in Madhya Pradesh dismissed the petition filed by the family of a Noida woman who was found dead at her marital residence in Bhopal, requesting a second post-mortem examina...

item.title: Tanzania Financial Sector Shows Strong Growth Across Banks, Capital Markets, and Social Security in 2025
item.description: In 2025, Tanzania's financial system demonstrated strong stability and growth, according to the Bank of Tanzania Financial Stability Report. The banking sector led the expansion with total assets incr...

item.title: Karachi Court Sentences Cleric for Sexual Abuse of Two Madressah Students
item.description: KARACHI: In a significant judgement, a sessions court in Karachi has sentenced Qari Muhammad Anwar, a cleric and madressah teacher, to two concurrent 15-year jail terms for sexually abusing two of his...
